div.h3: Dense Array Cache Load → Sequential Iteration → Sub-Millisecond Target

div.p: With archetype layout, compiler can auto-vectorize to process 4-8 entities per instruction (AVX2/AVX-512). Potential additional 2-4x speedup.
T = 0μs Benchmark Start
~16μs Cache Load
~200-400μs Iteration
<500μs Complete ✓
1000μs (1ms) Target Limit
Size: 800 KB (8 bytes per Position)
1 cache miss = 8 entities loaded
Dense Array: 800 KB L3 Cache: 8-32 MB (typical) Transfer: Sequential read Bandwidth: ~50 GB/s (DDR4) Time = 800KB / 50GB/s = 16 μs
┌─────────────────────────────┐ │ Position[0..99999] │ │ ████████████████████ 800KB │ └─────────────────────────────┘ ✓ Entire array fits in L3 ✓ Prefetcher activated ✓ Sequential access pattern
No indirection — direct array access No sparse lookup — contiguous memory
Time → Access: [0][1][2][3][4][5][6][7]... ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ Cache: ████████████████████████ ↑Prefetch↑ Each access triggers next-line prefetch. Cache lines arrive BEFORE they're needed.
Operations: 100,000 × 4 (2 mul, 2 add) = 400,000 FLOPs At 3 GHz: ~133 μs compute With overhead: L1 hits: ~4 cycles each Loop: ~10 cycles per iter Total: ~200-400 μs

┌────────────────────────┐ │ ✓ 100,000 entities │ │ ✓ 2 components each │ │ ✓ All positions updated│ │ │ │ Time: 200-400 μs │ │ Target: < 1000 μs │ │ │ │ MARGIN: 2-5x faster │ └────────────────────────┘
Sparse Set: ~1-2 ms Archetype: ~0.3 ms Speedup: 3-6x faster Why? • No sparse array lookup • Pure sequential access • SIMD auto-vectorization
0μs 500μs 1000μs │..........│...........│ ↑ ↑ Actual Target ~300μs 1000μs ✓ PASS with 3x margin
L1 Cache: 32 KB Latency: ~4 cycles Position fits: 4,096 entities
L2 Cache: 256 KB - 1 MB Latency: ~12 cycles Position fits: 32K-128K entities
L3 Cache: 8 - 32 MB Latency: ~40 cycles Position fits: 1M-4M entities ✓
RAM: 16+ GB Latency: ~100+ cycles 800KB sequential: ~16μs
